title: Hash as anonymization
description: A hashed or encoded identifier is treated as anonymous and sent to new sinks.
body: Failure mode: email, phone, user ID, or device ID is hashed and considered safe for analytics, sharing, or model training despite linkability and dictionary attacks. Detection signals include MD5/SHA of small-domain identifiers, stable hashes across systems, and joins on hashed values. Remediation is treat the value as pseudonymous personal data, minimize sharing, and evaluate linkability. Human gate is required for new external destinations. Use when a proposed approach skips personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion.
